title: Stromovka

infobox.value: This garden-related article is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Stromovka&action=edit).

This [arboretum](/wiki/Arboretum "Arboretum") article is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Stromovka&action=edit).

(**Royal Game Reserve**) is a large park in the [Bubeneč](/wiki/Bubene%C4%8D "Bubeneč") district of [Prague](/wiki/Prague "Prague"). It sits on the floodplain of the river [Vltava](/wiki/Vltava "Vltava"). At present, it spreads over an area of 95 hectares. It was established in the thirteenth century as a [game reserve](/wiki/Game_reserve "Game reserve") for the nearby summerhouse. At the beginning of the 19th century, it was converted into a park, which later in the 19th and 20th centuries was reduced by the construction of railways, the building of the [Academy of Fine Arts](/wiki/Academy_of_Fine_Arts,_Prague "Academy of Fine Arts, Prague") and the [Planetarium](/wiki/Planet%C3%A1rium_Praha "Planetárium Praha"), a ship canal, and last but not least by the construction of the [exhibition ground](/wiki/V%C3%BDstavi%C5%A1t%C4%9B_Praha "Výstaviště Praha").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The park is maintained as an [English landscape garden](/wiki/English_landscape_garden "English landscape garden"). It is protected as a [natural monument](/wiki/Natural_monument "Natural monument")[[2]](#cite_note-2) as well as a [cultural monument](/wiki/Cultural_monument_(Czech_Republic) "Cultural monument (Czech Republic)").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [50°06′23″N 14°25′10″E﻿ / ﻿50.1064°N 14.4194°E﻿ / 50.1064; 14.4194](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Stromovka&params=50.1064_N_14.4194_E_source:wikidata)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Park in Prague-Bubeneč, Czech Republic

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/54/Praha%2C_Bubene%C4%8D%2C_pohled_na_jez%C3%ADrko_ve_Stromovce.jpg/250px-Praha%2C_Bubene%C4%8D%2C_pohled_na_jez%C3%ADrko_ve_Stromovce.jpg)](/wiki/File:Praha,_Bubene%C4%8D,_pohled_na_jez%C3%ADrko_ve_Stromovce.jpg)

Pond in the park

**Stromovka** (**Royal Game Reserve**) is a large park in the [Bubeneč](/wiki/Bubene%C4%8D "Bubeneč") district of [Prague](/wiki/Prague "Prague"). It sits on the floodplain of the river [Vltava](/wiki/Vltava "Vltava"). At present, it spreads over an area of 95 hectares. It was established in the thirteenth century as a [game reserve](/wiki/Game_reserve "Game reserve") for the nearby summerhouse. At the beginning of the 19th century, it was converted into a park, which later in the 19th and 20th centuries was reduced by the construction of railways, the building of the [Academy of Fine Arts](/wiki/Academy_of_Fine_Arts,_Prague "Academy of Fine Arts, Prague") and the [Planetarium](/wiki/Planet%C3%A1rium_Praha "Planetárium Praha"), a ship canal, and last but not least by the construction of the [exhibition ground](/wiki/V%C3%BDstavi%C5%A1t%C4%9B_Praha "Výstaviště Praha").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The park is maintained as an [English landscape garden](/wiki/English_landscape_garden "English landscape garden"). It is protected as a [natural monument](/wiki/Natural_monument "Natural monument")[[2]](#cite_note-2) as well as a [cultural monument](/wiki/Cultural_monument_(Czech_Republic) "Cultural monument (Czech Republic)").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

A shopping mall named [Centrum Stromovka](/w/index.php?title=Centrum_Stromovka&action=edit&redlink=1 "Centrum Stromovka (page does not exist)") opened in 2019 adjacent to the park, in Veletržní street. Centrum Stromovka, named after the park, is a convenience-based shopping mall of 15,000 sq m and 84 units.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
